judges.value: Justice A.B. SHARMA JUSTICE C.D. PATEL

directives.directive_text:
While we are mindful of the economic interests and employment concerns raised by the respondent, the right to a
healthy environment must take precedence, in accordance with the polluter pays principle established by the Hon'ble
Supreme Court.
Page 2/3

IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA AT BENGALURU
ORDER
For the reasons aforesaid, and in exercise of the powers conferred under Article 226 of the Constitution of India, this
Court hereby passes the following order and directions:
directives.source_text:
While we are mindful of the economic interests and employment concerns raised by the respondent, the right to a
healthy environment must take precedence, in accordance with the polluter pays principle established by the Hon'ble
Supreme Court.
Page 2/3

IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA AT BENGALURU
ORDER
For the reasons aforesaid, and in exercise of the powers conferred under Article 226 of the Constitution of India, this
Court hereby passes the following order and directions:

directives.directive_text:
The Department of Environment, Government of Karnataka, shall submit a detailed environmental remediation
plan for the restoration of water quality in the Arkavathi River and surrounding groundwater sources within 60
days from the date of this order. The plan shall include specific timelines, cost estimates, and monitoring
mechanisms.
directives.source_text:
The Department of Environment, Government of Karnataka, shall submit a detailed environmental remediation
plan for the restoration of water quality in the Arkavathi River and surrounding groundwater sources within 60
days from the date of this order. The plan shall include specific timelines, cost estimates, and monitoring
mechanisms.

directives.directive_text:
The respondent, M/s Industrial Polluters Pvt. Ltd., is directed to immediately cease all discharge of untreated or
partially treated industrial effluents into the Arkavathi River or any water body forthwith. Any violation of this
direction shall attract contempt proceedings.
directives.source_text:
The respondent, M/s Industrial Polluters Pvt. Ltd., is directed to immediately cease all discharge of untreated or
partially treated industrial effluents into the Arkavathi River or any water body forthwith. Any violation of this
direction shall attract contempt proceedings.

directives.directive_text:
The respondent shall deposit a sum of Rs. 5,00,000 (Rupees Five Lakhs Only) as interim environmental
compensation with the Karnataka State Pollution Control Board within 30 days from the date of this order. This
amount shall be utilized exclusively for providing clean drinking water to the affected communities in Hesaraghatta
and surrounding villages.
directives.source_text:
The respondent shall deposit a sum of Rs. 5,00,000 (Rupees Five Lakhs Only) as interim environmental
compensation with the Karnataka State Pollution Control Board within 30 days from the date of this order. This
amount shall be utilized exclusively for providing clean drinking water to the affected communities in Hesaraghatta
and surrounding villages.

directives.directive_text:
The Commissioner of the Department of Social Welfare shall ensure that the affected communities are provided
with alternative clean drinking water supply through tanker services or temporary pipeline connections, provided
that the assessment report from the District Health Officer confirms groundwater contamination, within 90 days
from the date of this order.
conditions: the assessment report from the District Health Officer confirms groundwater contamination
directives.responsible_entity: Commissioner of the Department of Social Welfare
directives.source_text:
The Commissioner of the Department of Social Welfare shall ensure that the affected communities are provided
with alternative clean drinking water supply through tanker services or temporary pipeline connections, provided
that the assessment report from the District Health Officer confirms groundwater contamination, within 90 days
from the date of this order.

directives.directive_text:
The Superintendent of Police, Bengaluru Rural District, is directed to ensure strict compliance of this order and
shall file a compliance report before this Court on or before 15th June, 2024. Any obstruction in the execution of
this order shall be dealt with under the relevant provisions of the Indian Penal Code.
directives.main_action: file a compliance report before this Court on or before 15th June, 2024
directives.source_text:
The Superintendent of Police, Bengaluru Rural District, is directed to ensure strict compliance of this order and
shall file a compliance report before this Court on or before 15th June, 2024. Any obstruction in the execution of
this order shall be dealt with under the relevant provisions of the Indian Penal Code.

directives.directive_text:
The Karnataka State Pollution Control Board shall conduct monthly inspections of the respondent's unit and
submit quarterly compliance reports to this Court, subject to the condition that the first report shall be submitted
within 45 days.
conditions:
the condition that the first report shall be submitted
within 45 days
directives.source_text:
The Karnataka State Pollution Control Board shall conduct monthly inspections of the respondent's unit and
submit quarterly compliance reports to this Court, subject to the condition that the first report shall be submitted
within 45 days.

directives.directive_text:
The respondent shall complete the installation and commissioning of the new Effluent Treatment Plant within 6
months from the date of this order. The respondent shall not resume full-scale production operations until the new
ETP is certified as operational by the KSPCB.
The petition stands disposed of in the above terms. No costs. The Registry shall send a copy of this order to all the
respondents and concerned authorities forthwith.
Sd/- Sd/-
JUSTICE A.B. SHARMA JUSTICE C.D. PATEL
Pronounced in open court on this 15th day of March, 2024.
Page 3/3
